title: Elysium Health launches TELI — physician-led longevity institute scales NAD+ science to clinical care
description: Elysium Health announced The Elysium Longevity Institute (TELI), a science-first, physician-led, comprehensive longevity program led by new CMO Omar Baker MD with Lenny Guarente as chief scientist. Initial enrollment is capped at 10,000 with Q3 2026 launch — positioning Elysium to consolidate the NAD+ supplement franchise into a full healthspan-care vertical alongside Hone, Function, and the new Florida clinic build-out.

title: Bar-Ilan / NIA / Tel Aviv: SIRT6 overexpression reverses chromatin aging in old mouse livers
description: Cohen lab (Sagol Healthy Human Longevity Center, Bar-Ilan) plus NIA and Tel Aviv University published in Nature Communications that SIRT6 overexpression in old mice reversed age-associated chromatin accessibility changes and inflammation in liver — 'we took an old liver and restored its DNA organization toward a much younger state.' Builds on Cohen's 2012 SIRT6 lifespan-extension finding and feeds directly into SirTLab's translational program.

title: Meta-analysis: semaglutide cuts DunedinPACE 9%; NR, rapamycin, and D+Q senolytics show no signal in next-gen human clocks
description: Comprehensive review of interventions tested in next-generation epigenetic clocks finds semaglutide reduced DunedinPACE by 9% and PhenoAge by 4.9 years over 32 weeks; FTC/TAF cut PhenoAge by 6.33 years over 12 weeks. Conversely, nicotinamide riboside, rapamycin, and dasatinib/quercetin/fisetin senolytics produced no statistically significant reductions, hardening the case for metabolic and inflammation modulation over classical geroprotectors in human trials.

title: AGE 2026 unveils 2026 pipeline: Junevity siRNA reprogramming, BioAge Morgen, Rubedo GPX4 senolytic Phase 1
description: American Aging Association AGE 2026 Session 4 showcases the current translational lineup: Junevity's siRNA-based cell reprogramming, BioAge's Morgen program, and Rubedo Life Sciences' GPX4-modulating first-in-class selective senolytic now in Phase 1 for inflammatory skin and aging skin — confirming GPX4 as a third precision-senolytic mechanism behind apoptosis-induction and Bcl-xL classes.

title: Fight Aging consolidates aged CD8+ T cell / GZMK as druggable immune-aging node driving cognitive decline
description: New Fight Aging review of aged circulating CD8+ T cells and their secreted GZMK factor formalizes the immune-brain-aging axis as a near-term therapeutic target. Combined with this week's PM20D1/N-oleoyl-leucine microglia-amyloid clearance finding and the microglial senescence Alzheimer's consensus, dementia geroscience now has a coherent multi-axis intervention map.

title: Elysium Health announces launch of Elysium Longevity Institute (TELI)
description: Elysium Health launched TELI, a physician-led longevity program offering personalized supplement and prescription protocols, peptides, vitamin injections, and ongoing care across healthy aging, energy/performance, detox/inflammation, and metabolic health. Initial enrollment capped at 10,000; Q3 2026 launch; led by new CMO Omar Baker MD with Lenny Guarente as chief scientist.

title: Israeli scientists 'rewind' aging in old mouse livers by boosting SIRT6
description: Bar-Ilan University, National Institute on Aging, and Tel Aviv University published in Nature Communications that SIRT6 overexpression in old mice reversed age-related chromatin changes and inflammation in liver — restoring DNA organization toward a younger state. Builds on Cohen's 2012 SIRT6 lifespan finding; SirTLab pursuing therapeutic translation.

title: Rapamycin News meta-analysis: which interventions actually move next-gen human epigenetic clocks
description: Detailed meta-analysis ranks interventions tested in next-gen clocks: FTC/TAF (-6.33 years PhenoAge), omega-3 (-6.1 years DamAge in overweight cohort), semaglutide (-4.9 years PhenoAge, 9% DunedinPACE), umbilical cord plasma (-2.4 years DamAge), ketamine (-1.81 years OMICmAge). NR, rapamycin, and D+Q/fisetin senolytics failed to reach statistical significance — a major credibility update for the field.

title: AGE 2026 Session 4: A4LI policy, Junevity siRNA reprogramming, BioAge Morgen, Rubedo GPX4 senolytic Phase 1
description: American Aging Association AGE 2026 Session 4 highlights longevity translation: A4LI advancing longevity policy in Washington; Junevity siRNA cell reprogramming; BioAge Morgen; and Rubedo Life Sciences' GPX4 senolytic in Phase 1 for inflammatory skin/aging skin — confirming GPX4 modulation as a new precision-senolytic axis.

title: STAT Readout Loud: Eli Lilly gene-editing for high cholesterol, new longevity startup funding round, ASCO preview
description: Readout Loud weekly podcast features Jeremy Levin on biotech's strategic turning point and recaps an Eli Lilly gene-editing treatment for high cholesterol and a new funding round for a longevity startup ahead of the ASCO oncology meeting.

title: Fight Aging: senolytics improve dental pulp function — NFATC1 loss drives tooth aging
description: Researchers identify loss of NFATC1 activity in dental pulp mesenchymal stromal cells as a driver of tooth aging and report senolytics ameliorate pulpal degeneration and restore regenerative capacity — extending the precision senolytic indication map to dental aging.

title: PM20D1-derived N-oleoyl-leucine improves microglial amyloid-beta clearance in Alzheimer's models
description: PM20D1-derived metabolite N-oleoyl-leucine improves Alzheimer's pathology in animal models by promoting microglial association with amyloid-beta plaques, increasing clearance, and enhancing neuroprotection — a new endogenous lipid-mediator handle on neurodegeneration.

title: APOE2 reduces DNA damage and cellular senescence in human neurons
description: Study reports the APOE2 variant promotes DNA-signaling pathways that resist cellular senescence in human neurons, while APOE4 shows opposite pattern — mechanistic foundation for the APOE2 longevity-protective phenotype.

title: Aged circulating CD8+ T cells and GZMK drive hippocampal cognitive decline
description: Paper reports aged circulating CD8+ T cells and their secreted factor GZMK drive hippocampal aging signatures and cognitive decline — ameliorated by targeting these cells or GZMK — formalizing a druggable immune-aging-brain node.

title: Optimal cardiovascular protection may require ~560-610 minutes per week of MVPA
description: New analysis finds 150 min/week MVPA produces only modest cardiovascular risk reduction; ~560-610 min/week of moderate-to-vigorous physical activity is associated with substantially greater protection — likely upward revision of geroprotective exercise targets.

title: Scribe Therapeutics tests epigenetic treatment to lower cholesterol without altering DNA
description: Scribe Therapeutics is testing an epigenetic editing approach to durably lower cholesterol without altering the DNA sequence, joining Eli Lilly's gene-editing cholesterol program and Repair Biotechnologies' REP-0004 in the converging cardiovascular-aging therapeutics space.

title: Alzinova advances ALZ-101 vaccine to Phase 2 after positive safety results
description: Alzinova advanced ALZ-101 Alzheimer's vaccine to Phase 2 trial following positive Phase 1 safety results, adding another active-immunization approach to the neurodegeneration aging pipeline alongside AlzeCure ACD856 and tau-targeting therapies.

title: MBX Biosciences to share updates on peptide therapies for metabolic disorders
description: MBX Biosciences scheduled to share updates on its peptide therapy pipeline for metabolic disorders — extending the GLP-1-class era of metabolic geroprotection following semaglutide's strong epigenetic-clock signal.

title: Sky Labs CART BP pro cuff-less blood pressure device included in Korean hypertension guidelines
description: Sky Labs' CART BP pro included in Korean hypertension guidelines — a notable regulatory acceptance of continuous cuff-less BP monitoring as a clinical aging-cardiovascular tool.

title: Multiple biotechs race toward regulatory milestones for muscle-wasting (sarcopenia) therapies
description: Longevity.Technology highlights a competitive race among biotechs toward regulatory milestones for muscle-wasting disease therapies — sarcopenia emerging as the next major aging-indication category behind cognitive decline and atherosclerosis.

title: Absci ABS-201 hair loss candidate gets BTIG buy rating, $9 PT, $2.2B peak sales potential
description: BTIG initiated Absci with buy and $9 PT, calling ABS-201 — an AI-engineered long-lasting antibody for hair loss — a potential 'GLP-1 moment for hair loss' with up to $2.2B peak sales; an AI drug-discovery showcase outside oncology.

title: Daewoong acquires Turn Bio age-reversal assets — Asia's first major OSK reprogramming deal
description: Korean pharma Daewoong acquired Turn Biotechnologies' partial-epigenetic-reprogramming platform, marking Asia's first major M&A move into OSK reprogramming and accelerating partial-reprogramming concurrent with Life Biosciences ER-100 first dosing.

title: Tau-targeting drug slows early Alzheimer's progression
description: New tau-targeting therapy reported clinically meaningful slowing of early Alzheimer's progression — extending neurodegeneration pipeline beyond amyloid-centric approaches into a second major druggable mechanism.

title: Repair Biotechnologies REP-0004 mRNA-LNP atherosclerosis-reversal candidate detailed
description: Fight Aging interview details REP-0004 — liver-targeted mRNA lipid-nanoparticle therapy designed to reduce intracellular free cholesterol, regress atherosclerotic plaque, and reverse fatty liver disease — second damage-clearance modality alongside Cyclarity's UDP-003.

title: Tyshkovskiy/Gladyshev (Harvard) publish universal mammalian aging clocks
description: Universal biological aging markers shared across mammalian species provide a framework for screening interventions in mice and rapidly translating to humans — candidate-identification engine for lifespan-extending therapies.

title: Kirkland Foresight: 15 active senolytic trials including ALSENLITE, SToMP-AD, SENSURV starting June
description: James Kirkland (Mayo) detailed the active senolytic clinical network — 15 trials including ALSENLITE pilot, SToMP-AD multicenter Alzheimer's, NIH-funded SENSURV starting June, ONR osteoarthritis, and multiple COVID complications studies.

title: Sinclair: 'If the epigenome is reversible, so is aging' — Life Biosciences human trial begins
description: David Sinclair posts that Life Biosciences will begin testing age reversal in humans in the coming months and reiterates that cellular reprogramming will deliver an epigenetic reset reversing aging — concurrent with ER-100 glaucoma Phase 1 dosing.

title: Insilico + Human Longevity AI longevity foundation model collaboration
description: Insilico Medicine and Human Life Foundation Models (HLI subsidiary) signed multi-million-dollar collaboration to build industry-first AI foundation models for longevity science, combining Pharma.AI with HLI multi-omic clinical data.

title: Hallmarks of Aging dominant framework consolidating field — paradigms and progress paper
description: Philosophy-of-science analysis argues Hallmarks of Aging is now the field's dominant conceptual framework — structuring consensus and research priorities even amid ongoing theoretical disagreement on aging's root cause.
